lines: Nessa has already lined clean jars near the door, each one covered with cloth.
"The font is ruined. The cistern may be clean if they did not know where to look. I know where to look."
Nessa sits beside three stoppered jars and a knife.
"I kept the water ledger. Soldiers used to joke that I weighed every swallow. I did, too. Walls do not stand on prayers alone."
"No soldier has laughed here in weeks."
"Water makes liars honest. A man who says he drank one cup leaves two cups missing."
"The ledger did not make me beloved. Beloved people die thirsty first."
"Bad water joke. I keep the good ones for people who return jars."

choices.label: I will remember the jar law

lines: "The old cistern has a false lip under the third slab. Wardens hid a second draw there when the fever house still ran."
"The cult loved symbols more than plumbing. For once, their ignorance earned its keep."
"Do not touch it bare-handed. Do not let a child look in and ask why it shines."
"Holy water is only water until someone needs it to be more. Blood is worse. Blood always means itself first."
"I am sure of covered jars, boiled cloth, and knives kept clean."
"For seals, rope, and anyone who thinks water is free because it falls from the sky."
"It has never fallen free here. It falls with ash in it, and then I get blamed for the taste."
